Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a provided tabular event log of an Insurance Claims Processing process I need to identify cases with performance issues long durations analyze the root causes based on the attributes Resource Region Complexity explain why these attributes cause issues and propose mitigation strategies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Step by Step I need to calculate the total duration for each Case ID to identify the outliers long cases I also need to look at the number of steps specifically Request Additional Documents and track the attributes Resource Region Complexity for each case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes Attributes Resource CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Ann Finance Alan Region A Complexity Low Notes No additional documents requested Very fast

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration 26 hours 1 day 1 hour 55 minutes Attributes Resource CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl Region B Complexity Medium Notes 1 request for additional documents Delay between request 14 00 and approval next day 10 00

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration 48 hours 2 days 20 minutes Attributes Resource CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Bill Finance Alan Region A Complexity High Notes 2 requests for additional documents Long delay between 2nd request Apr 1 17 00 and approval Apr 2 16 00

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes Attributes Resource CSR Mary Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl Region B Complexity Low Notes No additional documents requested Very fast

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration 77 hours 3 days 5 hours 5 minutes Attributes Resource CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill Finance Carl Region B Complexity High Notes 3 requests for additional documents Significant delays between requests and final approval

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Addressing Task 1 Identify long cases Fast cases 2001 1 5 hrs 2004 1 25 hrs Both are Low complexity Moderately long case 2002 26 hrs Medium complexity 1 document request Significantly long cases 2003 48 hrs and 2005 77 hrs Both are High complexity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze Root Causes Addressing Task 2 Attribute correlation Complexity This is the most obvious driver High complexity 2003 2005 directly correlates with multiple Request Additional Documents events and longer overall durations Medium complexity 2002 has one request and a moderate delay Low complexity 2001 2004 has zero requests and is resolved in under 2 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource Adjuster Mike Case 2003 High and Adjuster Lisa Case 2005 High handle the complex cases with multiple document requests Manager Bill is the one approving the high complexity claims 2003 2005 and there seems to be a bottleneck between the final document request and his approval for example in 2005 last request is Apr 3 15 00 approval is Apr 4 10 00

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Region A has one fast 2001 and one slow 2003 case Region B has one fast 2004 one medium 2002 and one slow 2005 case Conclusion Region does not appear to be a primary root cause as both regions have fast and slow cases depending on complexity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Develop Explanations and Mitigations Addressing Task 3 Root Cause 1 High Complexity and Multiple Document Requests Explanation High complexity claims inherently require more scrutiny However the sequential nature of requesting documents for example Case 2005 has 3 separate requests over 3 days suggests the adjuster is reviewing piecemeal rather than asking for everything at once This creates a ping pong effect with the customer

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Mitigation Implement a comprehensive initial checklist for high complexity claims Train adjusters to identify all missing information during the first evaluation and send a single consolidated request

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root Cause 2 Resource Bottlenecks Specifically Manager Bill and Adjusters Explanation Manager Bill handles approvals for high complexity cases The gap between the final document submission and approval for example overnight or next day delays suggests a bottleneck at the managerial approval stage Adjusters might also be overwhelmed by high complexity cases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Mitigation Cross train managers for example Manager Ann to handle high complexity approvals to distribute the load Implement SLA Service Level Agreement alerts for claims pending approval for more than 24 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root Cause 3 Lack of Automation for Document Gathering Explanation Relying on manual requests delays the process significantly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Mitigation Introduce an automated customer portal where claimants can upload all standard and complex case documents upfront with AI validation checking for completeness before the claim even reaches an adjuster

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Format Ensure the response directly answers all three parts of the prompt Use clear headings bullet points and bold text for readability Double check the math on durations to ensure accuracy Case 2001 1 5h Case 2002 26h Case 2003 48h Case 2004 1 25h Case 2005 77h The math holds up

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Final Polish Make the tone professional analytical and actionable fitting the persona of a process analyst or operations manager Self Correction during drafting Make sure not to blame a specific region since the data does not support it but explicitly state why region is ruled out to show thorough analysis
